subject: Computer Networking
title: Undersea Cables
description: A quick, practical guide to Undersea Cables.

hook.text: There are over 500 cables on the ocean floor right now, carrying 99% of intercontinental data.

buildup.text: Each cable is a bundle of fiber optic strands, armored in steel and plastic, laid by specialized ships.

discovery.text: Repeaters every 50 miles boost the light signal so it can cross entire oceans without fading.

twist.text: Anchors, earthquakes, and even sharks have damaged cables. Redundancy is the only real protection.

climax.text: When a cable to Tonga was cut in 2022, the entire country lost internet for weeks.

punchline.text: The cloud isn't in the sky. It's under the sea.

quiz.question: What carries most intercontinental internet traffic?
